column1: "Accept Revocation" applies the revocation_accepted order status and opens the credit memo page. "Reject Revocation" applies the revocation_rejected status. Neither changes the order state, and no refund happens automatically.

column1:
EU Directive 2023/2673 (in Germany: §356a BGB) requires B2C shops selling into the EU to provide a revocation button from 19 June 2026. This module provides the legally required channel; the existing withdrawal information page and PDF withdrawal form remain mandatory and unchanged.

To display the button on the storefront, insert the Revocation Button widget into your footer (or any block that stays visible throughout the withdrawal period).
column2:
EU Directive 2023/2673 (in Germany: §356a BGB) requires B2C shops selling into the EU to provide a revocation button from 19 June 2026. This module provides the legally required channel; the existing withdrawal information page and PDF withdrawal form remain mandatory and unchanged.

To display the button on the storefront, insert the Revocation Button widget into your footer (or any block that stays visible throughout the withdrawal period).
